content.subject: software standard
virtual observatory
data access layer
DAL
content.description: The Simple Spectral Access (SSA) Protocol (SSAP) defines a uniform interface to remotely discover and access one dimensional spectra. SSA is a member of an integrated family of data access interfaces altogether comprising the Data Access Layer (DAL) of the IVOA. SSA is based on a more general data model capable of describing most tabular spectrophotometric data, including time series and spectral energy distributions (SEDs) as well as 1-D spectra; however the scope of the SSA interface as specified in this document is limited to simple 1-D spectra, including simple aggregations of 1-D spectra. The form of the SSA interface is simple: clients first query the global resource registry to find services of interest and then issue a data discovery query to selected services to determine what relevant data is available from each service; the candidate datasets available are described uniformly in a VOTable format document which is returned in response to the query. Finally, the client may retrieve selected datasets for analysis. Spectrum datasets returned by an SSA spectrum service may be either precomputed, archival datasets, or they may be virtual data which is computed on the fly to respond to a client request. Spectrum datasets may conform to a standard data model defined by SSA, or may be native spectra with custom project-defined content. Spectra may be returned in any of a number of standard data formats. Spectral data is generally stored externally to the VO in a format specific to each spectral data collection; currently there is no standard way to represent astronomical spectra, and virtually every project does it differently. Hence spectra may be actively mediated to the standard SSA-defined data model at access time by the service, so that client analysis programs do not have to be familiar with the idiosyncratic details of each data collection to be accessed. Note that the metadata used to describe an SSA service in the registry is defined in the SimpleDALRegExt standard (ivo://www.ivoa.net/std/SimpleDALRegExt; http://www.ivoa.net/Documents/SimpleDALRegExt/).

relationship.relatedResource: SimpleDALRegExt: Describing Simple Data Access Services

param.name: REQUEST
param.description: The request or operation name, where only "queryData" is allowed as a value.

param.name: VERSION
param.description: The version of the SSA standard this query requests support for.

param.name: POS
param.description: Search Position in the form "ra,dec" where ra and dec are given in decimal degrees. The coordinate system defaults to ICRS; otherwise, the system can be specified by appending ";sys", where sys is the name of the desired standard reference frame as defined in the STC standard.

param.name: SIZE
param.description: The diameter of the search cone specified in decimal degrees.

param.name: BAND
param.description: The desired spectral bandpass given either by a name or a frequency range.

param.name: BAND
param.description: The desired spectral bandpass given either by a name or a frequency range.

param.name: TIME
param.description: The desired time coverage (epoch) given as a time range.

param.name: FORMAT
param.description: A comma-delimited list of requested formats, each specified as a MIME-type or one of "all", "compliant", "native", "graphic", "votable", "fits", "xml", or "metadata".

param.name: APERTURE
param.description: diameter of the effective aperature to use when deriving spectra.

param.name: SPECRP
param.description: The minimum spectral resolution, specified as the spectral resolving power l/dl in dimensionless units.

param.name: SPATRES
param.description: minimum spatial resolution (corresponding to the PSF of the observed signal) specified in decimal degrees.

param.name: TIMERES
param.description: The minimum time resolution, specified in seconds.

param.name: SNR
param.description: The minimum signal-to-noise ratio of a candidate dataset, for example specified as the ratio of the mean signal to the RMS noise of the background.

param.name: REDSHIFT
param.description: The photometric (observed) redshift range to restrict search to, specified as a single element open or closed range-list

param.name: VARAMPL
param.description: The acceptable range of variability amplitude, specified as a single element open or closed range-list, with values in the range 0.0 to 1.0.

param.name: TARGETNAME
param.description: The target name to search for, suitable for input to a source name resolver.

param.name: TARGETCLASS
param.description: A comma delimited list of strings denoting the types of astronomical objects to be searched for.

param.name: FLUXCALIB
param.description: The minimum level of flux calibration for acceptable data, given as one of “absolute”, "relative", "normalized", or “any” (the default).

param.name: WAVECALIB
param.description: the minimum level of spectral coordinate calibration for acceptable data, given as one of “absolute”, "relative", or "any" (the default).

param.name: PUBDID
param.description: The dataset identifier, as assigned by the publisher, to restrict to.

param.name: CREATORDID
param.description: The dataset identifier, as assigned by the creator, to restrict to.

param.name: COLLECTION
param.description: the name of the collection to restrict the search to

param.name: TOP
param.description: The maximum number of records to return, selecting the best matched records.

param.name: MAXREC
param.description: The maximum number of records to be returned.

param.name: MTIME
param.description: The time range that the dataset was last modified or deleted to restrict the results to.

param.name: COMPRESS
param.description: if present, return compressed versions of the datasets if available.

param.name: RUNID
param.description: the opaque string to identify the logical collection of related queries.
